name: Leiro – loop via Avia river, San Amaro and Monastery of San Clodio de Leiro
name_original: Leiro – vuelta por río Avia, San Amaro y Mosteiro de San Clodio de Leiro
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Leiro are situated in the province of Ourense, Galicia, Spain, characterized by diverse landscapes. The region features river valleys, notably the Avia River, and a hilly interior with significant elevation changes. Vineyards are a prominent feature, particularly in the O Ribeiro wine region where Leiro is located. This topography provides varied terrain for touring cyclists seeking engaging experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Leiro The most popular touring cycling route is O Carballiño, Maside, and Cea Loop, a 20.6 miles (33.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates through several towns with notable elevation gain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Leiro – loop via Avia river, San Amaro and Monastery of San Clodio de Leiro, a difficult 17.2 miles (27.7 km) path. This route follows the Avia River and includes a historical monastery. Local touring cyclists also love the San Clodio Medieval Bridge – Monastery of San Clodio loop from Leiro, a 33.5 miles (53.9 km) trail leading through river valleys and past historical sites, often completed in about 4 hours 12 minutes. Touring cycling around Leiro is defined by river valleys, hilly terrain, and vineyards. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 9 moderate and 26 difficult routes. The routes in Leiro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Leiro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Leiro?
A: The touring cycling routes around Leiro are characterized by diverse landscapes, including river valleys, notably the Avia River, and a hilly interior with significant elevation changes. You'll also find routes passing through vineyards in the O Ribeiro wine region. This varied topography offers an engaging experience for cyclists, from scenic riverside paths to challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly touring cycling routes in Leiro?
A: While Leiro's routes are generally known for their challenging terrain, with 26 out of 35 routes classified as difficult, there are still 9 moderate options available. These moderate routes offer a less strenuous experience while still allowing you to explore the region's beauty.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes in the Leiro area incorporate historical and cultural sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018462767/leiro-loop-via-avia-river-san-amaro-and-monastery-of-san-clodio-de-leiro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leiro – loop via Avia river, San Amaro and Monastery of San Clodio de Leiro&lt;/a&gt; route passes directly by the historic Monastery of San Clodio. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40007120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Clodio Medieval Bridge – Monastery of San Clodio loop from Leiro&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through river valleys and past historical sites, including the medieval bridge.

Q: Are there any routes that feature natural attractions like waterfalls or thermal springs?
A: Yes, the wider Ourense province, where Leiro is located, is known for its natural beauty. You can find routes that lead to natural attractions such as waterfalls and thermal springs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6952177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs – Millennium Bridge loop from O Carballiño&lt;/a&gt; offers the chance to visit thermal springs. Nearby natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantiño Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Melón&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6067935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mestas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Leiro?
A: Galicia generally offers pleasant cycling conditions from spring through autumn. The region experiences a mild climate, but it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts, especially during the rainy season. Spring and early autumn often provide comfortable temperatures and lush landscapes, ideal for touring.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Leiro are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017446434/o-carballino-maside-and-cea-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Carballiño, Maside, and Cea Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018462767/leiro-loop-via-avia-river-san-amaro-and-monastery-of-san-clodio-de-leiro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leiro – loop via Avia river, San Amaro and Monastery of San Clodio de Leiro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about their experience in Leiro?
A: The touring cycling routes in Leiro are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have explored the area using komoot, often praising the varied terrain and scenic beauty of the river valleys and vineyards.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through vineyards or wine regions?
A: Absolutely. Leiro is situated in the O Ribeiro region, which is renowned for its wine production. Many touring cycling routes will take you through picturesque vineyards, offering a chance to experience the local viticulture, especially in areas like Ribeira Sacra, known for its 'heroic viticulture' on terraced slopes.

Q: Are there opportunities to combine cycling with relaxation at thermal springs?
A: Yes, Ourense is famous for its thermal springs. Some cycling routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6952177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs – Millennium Bridge loop from O Carballiño&lt;/a&gt;, allow you to incorporate stops at these natural hot springs for relaxation, blending active tourism with wellness. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantes Thermal Park&lt;/a&gt; is also a nearby option.

Q: How long do the touring cycling routes typically take to complete?
A: The duration of routes varies depending on their distance and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017446434/o-carballino-maside-and-cea-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Carballiño, Maside, and Cea Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 33.1 km, typically takes around 2 hours and 13 minutes. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40007120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Clodio Medieval Bridge – Monastery of San Clodio loop from Leiro&lt;/a&gt; (53.9 km) can take about 4 hours and 12 minutes.

Q: Are there any specific natural monuments or unique geological features to explore?
A: Yes, the region boasts unique natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5026945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pena Corneira&lt;/a&gt;, a striking natural monument, is located in the vicinity and adds to the dramatic natural scenery. The broader Ourense province also features significant rivers like the Miño and Sil, with the Sil Canyon offering spectacular views.
tips: Seen from the road, National Historical Artistic Monument the monastery dates from the 6th century
Beautiful ascent by road without traffic full of winding curves and abundant shade; asphalt in good condition
Crossing the medieval bridge of San Clodio over the Avia River on your route between Leiro and Ribadavia. The three-arched masonry bridge spanning 85 m was built between 1496 and 1498.
